description: The pie chart only displays 6 of the 15 countries, but each slice's percentage is calculated relative to the total of all 15 countries — so the displayed percentages sum to only about 40% instead of 100%, leaving readers to wonder where the remaining ~60% went. Additionally, a pie chart is fundamentally inappropriate here because these are independent percentage values (each country's share of parliamentary seats), not parts of a single whole — the countries' values do not sum to any meaningful total. This misrepresents the data as if the six countries collectively account for only a fraction of some shared pool. [NOTE: Generated code failed to execute]
